Both findings traced to the same root cause: operator direct-pushes today (commits 0b9f6c01 / 35e5be71 / 9e47d500) bypassed build.js publish, and workspace-sync is FUSE→git only with no git→FUSE pull path. Mode 2 procedure applied: backfill FUSE from git canonical for all 7 files, then write report. Disk audit was secondary trigger (project footprint 389MB <500MB threshold; root partition 95% but that is sandbox baseline per project-relative measurement rule).

mode_2_infrastructure_and_fuse.root_cause: operator direct-pushes via Git Data API (commits 0b9f6c01 07:33Z, 35e5be71 13:09Z, 9e47d500 16:59Z) AND decider commit 4e79507c at 13:23Z. workspace-sync hourly cycles 08-16 did not pull these into FUSE because workspace-sync is FUSE→git universal-pusher only — there is no git→FUSE pull step for files modified externally to the FUSE workspace. build.js publish DOES copy git-owned files to FUSE when decider runs it, but the operator's Git Data API path skips build.js entirely; decider's 13:13Z run did execute build.js publish but apparently did not refresh build-scripts/generate-html.js to FUSE either (worth a follow-up investigation).

findings.title: FUSE-vs-git divergence on operator direct-push pattern (recurring)
findings.evidence: Today: 7 files diverged between FUSE workspace and git main HEAD. Same files were authored by 4 commits in the last ~10h: 0b9f6c01 (07:33Z operator close ISS-2094/2112/708), 4e79507c (13:23Z decider c5 patches), 35e5be71 (13:09Z operator DIRECTIVE-20260525-002 one-liners), 9e47d500 (16:59Z operator PROP-058+059 apply). Workspace-sync ran hourly through this window (08, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16) and pushed cleanly FUSE→git via universal-pusher mtime-guard, but never pulled git→FUSE for these paths.
findings.root_cause: workspace-sync is one-direction (FUSE→git) by design. build.js publish handles the inverse path (git→FUSE for git-owned files) only when decider/agents call it from a fresh clone. Operator Git Data API pushes (push-via-api.js) skip build.js, so changes never propagate to FUSE. Decider's 4e79507c at 13:23Z DID run build.js publish but somehow did not refresh generate-html.js — possibly a publish-flow gap worth investigating.
findings.impact_assessed: moderate — agents that clone fresh (decider, curmudgeon, analyst, analyst-baby, workspace-sync, prune-integrity) are unaffected because they always read git canonical. Tinker's Mode 1 stranded-patches check reads FUSE directly and produced a false positive today (1 file misreported as actionable). New Cowork sessions that auto-load FUSE CLAUDE.md as project context inherit the stale agent-table prose. Mode 1 false-positives are the main observable harm; the rest is consistency drift.
findings.remediation_applied_this_run: Backfilled all 7 files from raw.githubusercontent.com canonical content. Verified md5 match post-write. Cleaned up 7 .tinker-backfill-bak safety backups via mcp__cowork__allow_cowork_file_delete operator grant.
findings.recommended_structural_fix_as_prop: Add a git→FUSE pull step at the start of each workspace-sync run for paths NOT in the workspace-owned authoritative set. Pseudo: `for path in git_changed_since_last_sync; do if not is_workspace_authoritative(path): cp clone/$path FUSE/$path; done`. Already-existing fresh-clone Step 2 has the content; just need to add the reverse copy where workspace-owned guard is false. Smaller version: at minimum, sync stranded-patches-*.json tombstones git→FUSE so tinker Mode 1 stops producing false positives; the bigger fix is general.
findings.prop_priority: medium — non-blocking but recurring; symptoms compound silently
findings.related_prop_history: PROP-013 (NEVER_PUSH strand tracking) covered the FUSE→git stuck direction; this is the symmetric git→FUSE stuck direction. PROP-045 anti-reversion check is unrelated but in the same code path.

findings.title: 3 issues aged on assigned-tinker queue
findings.evidence: ISS-1285 (minor, 34d): raw-text/02-wins.txt poller-refresh hygiene. ISS-2134 (major, 6d): WIN-070 re-push loop fix (b) — decider dedup check before win-new push, only fix (a) git rm was applied per ISS-2126. ISS-2175 (moderate, 2d): EXP-425 stale pending >48h, linked to ISS-2105 86-EXP-gap audit (assigned-analyst).
findings.impact_assessed: ISS-2134 is the most actionable — implementing the dedup check in decider's win-new push handler is a prompt-level change. The previous re-push loop happened 3 times pre-fix-(a); since fix-(a) we have not observed a recurrence, but the load-bearing prevention (dedup check) remains absent. ISS-1285 is genuinely low-priority hygiene. ISS-2175 is blocked on ISS-2105 progress.
findings.recommendation: Schedule ISS-2134 (decider dedup-check) into a future Mode 4 tinker run when bandwidth permits. ISS-2175 should be re-routed to analyst if ISS-2105 has any progress, otherwise marked deferred. ISS-1285 can be closed as deferred-hygiene if poller raw-text refresh schedule won't change in the foreseeable future.

pre_existing_clone_observations.tinker_could_not_remove: different uid permissions blocked rm; not in tinker's clone-cleanup empowerment because mtime <2h excludes /tmp/edit-clone special case
pre_existing_clone_observations.recommendation: operator may want to investigate why /tmp/edit-clone is being created with uid nobody — likely a stale leftover from a previous scheduled agent that ran without proper umask/uid setup. Likely candidates: workspace-sync, prune-integrity, or one of the curmudgeon variants. If it persists, an operator-side rm at session boundary would clear it.

second_round_backfill.trigger: Commit 25c6de8c at 17:09Z moved git HEAD forward for these 3 files AFTER the first backfill (17:04Z). Without a second pass, FUSE would have been stale again within minutes of completing the first pass.
second_round_backfill.outcome: All 7 originally-stale files now match git HEAD as of 17:13Z. Demonstrates FND-01 in real-time: with no git→FUSE pull path, any commit that lands while a tinker run is in flight leaves FUSE behind.
